 {"id":"bd-atkc6","title":"fuzz: add fuzz_ext4_image_reader target for Ext4ImageReader","description":"ffs_ondisk::Ext4ImageReader (ext4.rs:3466) provides high-level image reading API (new, read_group_desc, read_inode, read_symlink) but lacks fuzz coverage. Create target that exercises full reader workflow on arbitrary images.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T13:05:24.255266803Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T23:41:58.553833127Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T23:41:58.553833127Z","close_reason":"Added fuzz_ext4_image_reader with seeded clean/mutated/truncated/raw image modes exercising Ext4ImageReader open, group descriptor, inode, block, directory lookup, and fast/extent-mapped symlink workflows; verified with rch cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin fuzz_ext4_image_reader and rch cargo clippy --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin fuzz_ext4_image_reader -- -D warnings","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-aw9l8","title":"[MEDIUM] vendored fuser dead-code warnings are leaking into workspace gates","description":"# Review finding

Both workspace gates emit warnings from the vendored `fuser` dependency before project crates run. The current run surfaced dead-code warnings in:
- `vendor/fuser/src/ll/fuse_abi.rs`
- `vendor/fuser/src/ll/request.rs`

## Warnings seen in current run
- `cuse_init_in` is never constructed
- `cuse_init_out` is never constructed
- `fuse_ioctl_iovec` is never constructed
- `fuse_notify_retrieve_out` is never constructed
- `fuse_notify_retrieve_in` is never constructed
- method `unrestricted` is never used

## Why this matters
These are not the current hard stop because `ffs-tui` fails clippy first, but they still pollute the mandated workspace `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` output. Once the `ffs-tui` lifetime issue is fixed, this vendored warning inventory may become the next gate blocker under `-D warnings`.

## Repro
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`

## Expected fix direction
Decide whether the vendored `fuser` code should carry explicit `#[allow(dead_code)]` for intentionally-unused ABI structs, or whether the vendored revision should be adjusted so workspace warning hygiene remains stable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T07:49:39.543516661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T07:49:39.543516661Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuser","qa","review","vendor","workspace-gate"]}
+{"id":"bd-aw9l8","title":"[MEDIUM] vendored fuser dead-code warnings are leaking into workspace gates","description":"# Review finding

Both workspace gates emit warnings from the vendored `fuser` dependency before project crates run. The current run surfaced dead-code warnings in:
- `vendor/fuser/src/ll/fuse_abi.rs`
- `vendor/fuser/src/ll/request.rs`

## Warnings seen in current run
- `cuse_init_in` is never constructed
- `cuse_init_out` is never constructed
- `fuse_ioctl_iovec` is never constructed
- `fuse_notify_retrieve_out` is never constructed
- `fuse_notify_retrieve_in` is never constructed
- method `unrestricted` is never used

## Why this matters
These are not the current hard stop because `ffs-tui` fails clippy first, but they still pollute the mandated workspace `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` output. Once the `ffs-tui` lifetime issue is fixed, this vendored warning inventory may become the next gate blocker under `-D warnings`.

## Repro
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`

## Expected fix direction
Decide whether the vendored `fuser` code should carry explicit `#[allow(dead_code)]` for intentionally-unused ABI structs, or whether the vendored revision should be adjusted so workspace warning hygiene remains stable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T07:49:39.543516661Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T01:27:21.000000000Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T01:27:21.000000000Z","close_reason":"Added targeted #[allow(dead_code)] annotations for intentionally-unused vendored fuser ABI structs and IoCtl::unrestricted, then verified with rch cargo check -p fuser and rch cargo clippy -p fuser -- -D warnings.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuser","qa","review","vendor","workspace-gate"]}
 {"id":"bd-b182","title":"Add ext4 rename test: directory over non-empty directory returns ENOTEMPTY","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:13:57.126732789Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T20:22:18.469222777Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T20:22:18.469014021Z","close_reason":"Added ext4 rename test: directory over non-empty directory returns ENOTEMPTY. Verifies source dir, dest dir, and dest children all survive the failed rename. Passes alongside existing btrfs equivalent.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-b25w4","title":"add mounted-path E2E xattr coverage for btrfs RW operations","description":"Survey finding: fuse_e2e.rs has extensive ext4 xattr mounted-path tests (py_setxattr, getxattr, listxattr) but btrfs mounted-path xattr coverage is limited. The unit tests exist in ffs-core but real FUSE round-trip proof is missing.

Scope:
- Add btrfs_rw_setxattr_getxattr E2E test in fuse_e2e.rs
- Test user.* namespace set/get/list cycle over mounted btrfs FUSE path
- Test security.* namespace if permissions allow
- Verify Create/Replace mode semantics match ext4 behavior
- Add btrfs_rw_removexattr E2E test for xattr deletion
- Emit SCENARIO_RESULT markers for CI visibility

Acceptance:
- At least 3 mounted-path btrfs xattr scenarios with deterministic assertions
- Tests soft-skip gracefully if mount fails (CI-safe)
- SCENARIO_RESULT markers in logs prove coverage in CI artifacts","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"pane-3-gemini-reviewer","created_at":"2026-04-21T11:23:16.691939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T12:40:10.578466659Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T12:40:10.575667781Z","close_reason":"Added SCENARIO_RESULT markers to all 8 pre-existing btrfs rw xattr E2E tests (set_get_list_remove, multiple_attributes, on_directory, create_existing→EEXIST, replace_missing→ENODATA, overwrite_value, get_nonexistent, remove_nonexistent) with btrfs_rw_xattr_<name> ids + invariant-specific detail strings. Markers emit only when the test actually runs (inside with_btrfs_rw_mount closure), so soft-skips stay distinguishable from PASS in CI artifacts. Build clean. sha=451ac40 on main+master.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
